answers.Q1_one_liner: Expense tracker that answers 'where did my money go?' at item + category level — not just 'which store' — so users see spending habits.
answers.Q2_primary_user: Individuals and families managing personal finances (breadwinners, earners). Excludes businesses of any size. Today's alternatives: (a) existing apps with slow entry + no item-level scan + poor navigation; (b) Excel with skill barrier.
answers.Q3_why_now: Attention economy — short spans paired with info hunger — demands fast, glanceable accountability. 'Gravity-center' detection: surface where spending concentrates within the user's own data.
answers.Q4_success_shape: Observable: (1) user can name top item categories hurting spend; (2) user identifies forgotten/surprise recurring charges; (3) [override-01] user contrasts own habits vs anonymized cohort — scheduled as late-phase capability.
answers.Q5_anti_vision: Explicitly excluded: income/investment tracking, tax features, business/corporate budgeting, contract-revision recommendations, kitchen/pantry inventory (Gustify). In-scope addition: item-level urgency/special-case flag (non-tax).

follow_up_answers.FQ1_sharing_model: H — single-user MVP, data model designed so shared-household can slot in later.

signals.differentiator_item_level: Scan receipts → item-level capture beyond store-level (V4 taxonomy 86 categories).
signals.differentiator_gravity_centers: Highlight concentration/anomaly in own spend over time.
signals.friction_target: Replace form-filling and Excel with scan + auto-parse.
signals.privacy_posture: Non-tax, non-business, personal-context only; benchmarking requires consent + anonymization.
signals.geography: Global from day 1 — Chile + LATAM + EU + US + Canada (override-02).
signals.currency_model: USD-shadow-minor on every transaction/item (ADR D6 preserved); local currency authoritative, USD for cross-market analytics.
signals.compliance_footprint: Chilean Law 21.719 + GDPR (EU) + PIPEDA (Canada) + US state-law baseline (CCPA/CPRA).
signals.deliverable_surfaces: THREE clients on shared FastAPI backend — (a) responsive web portal [NOT PWA], (b) native Android app, (c) native iOS app (override-03).
signals.structured_boleta_shortcut: QR/CAF parse of Chilean electronic boletas = late-phase nice-to-have, not MVP.
signals.statement_reconciliation: Two ingestion modes: receipts + credit card/bank statements. Worker reconciles statement lines against receipt-sourced transactions. Three buckets: matched / statement-only / receipt-only. Coverage metric = % of statement spend with matched receipt.
signals.card_privacy_posture: Card aliases ONLY (e.g., 'Santander Visa'). No card numbers, CVV, PAN fragments, expiry. PCI-regulated data never enters the system.
